member.name: Jake Richards
member.synopsis: Jake Richards is the Labour MP for Rother Valley , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently holds the Government posts of Assistant Whip, and Parliamentary Under-Secretary (Ministry of Justice).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Resettlement: Afghanistan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many people have been granted entry clearance to the UK under the (a) Afghan Relocations and Assistance Policy, (b) Afghan citizens resettlement scheme and (c) Afghanistan Response Route.
writtenQuestions.answerText: From 2021 to the end of June 2025, there have been around 36,000 arrivals under the Afghan Resettlement Programme, of which 13,200 were through the Afghan Citizens Resettlement Scheme, 19,000 through Afghan Relocation and Assistance Policy and 3,400 throu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Telephone Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress he has made on implementing 24/7 mental health text lines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Mental health crisis text services are an important part of delivering accessible and effective mental health support across the country. NHS England has confirmed that integrated care boards have plans to have 24 hour a day, seven day a week mental healt...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Office: Armed Forces Covenant
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of extending the Armed Forces Covenant Duty across her Departmental responsibilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office greatly values the contribution of all service personnel and remains committed to upholding its moral obligations under the Armed Forces Covenant.The Department already embeds the principles of the Covenant, specifically within the provisi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department of Health and Social Care: Armed Forces Covenant
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of extending the Armed Forces Covenant Duty across his Departmental responsibilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is supportive of the extension of the Armed Forces Covenant. A key benefit of the Department having a legal duty to have due regard to the principles of the Armed Forces Covenant is greater awareness and consistency of the unique impacts of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Benefit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what the cost to the public purse would be to not claim back child benefit from parents who have lost a child in the year after the upfront payment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon member to the answer given on 24 January to UIN 25010 regarding estimated costs of not claiming back Child Benefit overpayments.

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Benefit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what her Department's policy is on claiming back child benefit from parents who have lost a child in the year after the upfront payment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon member to the answer given on 23 January to UIN 25009 regarding the policy on Child Benefit overpayments.

writtenQuestions.heading: Marcus Fakana
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps his Department is taking to (a) support and (b) help secure the early release of Marcus Fakana.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Supporting the welfare of British nationals detained overseas is a priority for the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO). The FCDO cannot interfere in the judicial affairs of other countries, however where there are concerns that an individ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an estimate of the cost to her Department of not claiming back child benefit from parents who have lost a child in the year since the original payment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The estimated cost of not claiming back Child Benefit payments from parents who have lost a child in the year since the payment was made is not readily available and could only be produced at disproportionate cost.

writtenQuestions.heading: Child Benefit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what her Department's policy is on claiming back child benefit from parents who have lost a child in the year since the payment was made.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Entitlement to Child Benefit continues for eight weeks after the date of a child’s death. This recognises that the period following such a traumatic event is particularly difficult and avoids causing additional distress from the immediate removal of finan...

writtenQuestions.heading: Productivity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to increase economic productivity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Boosting productivity growth across the country is a key aim of this government’s growth mission. By reforming the economy and increasing productivity, we will drive up prosperity and living standards across the UK. Our Budget showed that this government ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Migrant Workers: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how the money raised from the Immigration Skills Charge has been spent over the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Immigration Skills Charge is administered by the Home Office and is classified as Trust Statement income. Government departments are required to surrender all Trust Statement income to the Consolidated Fund (CF). Receipts surrendered to the CF are not...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how many Government services have moved to the GOV.UK One Login system; and what his planned timetable is for moving all Government services to this system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of November 2024, users can access 50 government services within GOV.UK One Login. Approximately 100 services are expected to be onboarded in 2025, with more to follow thereafter. Government departments are actively developing delivery plans to onboard...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Licences: Digital Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what progress she has made on the introduction of digital drivers licences.

writtenQuestions.heading: Crown Court: Opening Hours
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether she has made an assessment of the effectiveness of the process to determine the number of sitting days in the Crown Court in each financial year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Every year, the Government and the Judiciary agree a number of sitting days and an overall budget in what is known as the Concordat process.In June, the judiciary reached an agreement with the former Lord Chancellor to sit 106,000 days in the Crown Court ...
